text: Debugging is an essential skill for any programmer. The first step is identifying bugs by observing unexpected behaviour in your code. A systematic approach helps you find issues efficiently: start by reproducing the problem, then isolate the possible causes. Modern debugging tools make this process much easier. Most programming languages have built-in debuggers that let you pause execution and inspect variables. Logging is another powerful strategy—adding print statements or log messages at key points can reveal what your code is actually doing. By combining these techniques, you can move from guessing to understanding, fixing bugs methodically rather than randomly changing code.
